title: Habenaria tridentata
intro: Habenaria tridentata, also known as Tridentate Habenaria, is a species of orchid native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has shown that it may offer health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate conditions like fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria tridentata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Habenaria tridentata include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and are also valued for their potential anti-aging and anti-cancer properties.
taxonomy: Habenaria tridentata is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Within this family, it is classified under the subfamily Orchidoideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Orchideae and the subtribe Orchidinae. This species is also commonly referred to as the 'three- toothed Habenaria' in some taxonomic classifications.
